summary: I was born on the West Coast of Africa, which shaped my early childhood before I had to leave due to a civil war in my country. Growing up there was very different from my later life, but it remains an important part of my identity. The conflict forced me to move, which was a difficult but necessary decision.

I moved to the United States because of the civil war back home, and it was a major turning point in my life. Adjusting to a new country was challenging, but it also gave me many opportunities that I am grateful for. The U.S. became the place where I could build a new future after leaving West Africa.

After arriving in the United States, I grew up in Massachusetts, which was a big change from West Africa. Living there helped me adapt to American culture and build a foundation for my education and future. Massachusetts became my home during those formative years.

I attended Gustavus Adolphus College, which was an important step in my educational journey. Going to college there helped me develop skills and opened many doors for my future. My time at Gustavus Adolphus College was a key part of my success after moving to the United States.
